LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT SAN FRANCISCO UNIFIED SCHOOL DISTRICT'S DEMURRER TO PLAINTIFF'S 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT - THE DEMURRER TO THE DISABILITY DISCRIMINATION CAUSE OF ACTION IS OVERRULED. THE COURT LIBERALLY CONSTRUES THE COMPLAINT PURSUANT TO CCP 452. THE COURT CAN INFER THAT DEFENDANT HAD KNOWLEDGE OF THE DISABILITY BASED ON PLAINTIFF'S ALLEGATIONS ABOUT THE WORKER'S COMPENSATION CLAIM. THE COMPLAINT STATES A CAUSE OF ACTION AND DEFENDANT IS RAISING A FACTUAL ISSUE. AVILA V. CONTINENTAL AIRLINES, INC. (2008) 165 CAL.APP.4TH 1237, 1248, IS DISTINGUISHABLE. THAT WAS NOT A PLEADING CASE AND IT ADDRESSED THE PROPRIETY OF GRANTING SUMMARY JUDGMENT. THE DEMURRER TO THE RETALIATION CAUSE OF ACTION IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS CONCEDED. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: ERNEST GOLDSMITH. CLERK: ROSIE NOGUERA. REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR #5976. (302/EG)

DEFENDANT SAN FRANCISCO UNIFIED SCHOOL DISTRICT's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is
Off calendar. Notice of settlement of entire case filed April 27, 2017. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Carol Karen CSR #8189 =(302/HEK)

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Sep-28-2016 Continued To Jun-05-17 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD
Off Calendar Sep-12-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Attorney, Arkady Igor Itkin, Esq. (415-640-6765) appears as moving party/withdrawing counsel for plaintiff

Attorney, Eugene B. Elliot of Bertrand Fox Elliot Osman & Wenzel (415-353-0999) appears as counsel for defendant/opposing party San Francisco Unified School District

Attorney, Timothy J. Eagan, Esq. (707-789-9018) makes special appearance as counsel representing plaintiff, Yvelise Cabral in unrelated proceedings, not substituting into this action.

The cause is argued and submitted by both sides as set forth in the transcript of the certified shorthand reporter.

Attorney Arkady I. Itkin'S MOTION TO BE RELIEVED AS COUNSEL FOR PLAINTIFF YVELISE CABRAL is GRANTED. Mr. Itkin is directed to submit a [proposed] judicial counsel written order in compliance with CRC 3.1312. The written order must include notice of new trial date, any other pending dates and contact information for plaintiff, Ms. Cabral. Plaintiff, Ms. Cabral has 60 days to find new counsel.

Defendant counsel's request to vacate trial date and set new case management conference is GRANTED in part. The January 17, 2017 trial date is ordered continued to June 5, 2017 at 9:30 AM in Department 206. Defendant's counsel must submit a [proposed] written order in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Defendant counsel's oral request to set deadline for plaintiff, Ms. Cabral's deposition is GRANTED. Plaintiff is given 90 days to submit to deposition. Defendant's counsel must submit a [proposed] written order in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Accordingly, the Court is expecting two [proposed] written orders, an orderd continuing trial and related discovery deadlines and an order relieving Mr. Itkin as counsel.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Jose Rios Merida; Reporter: Sherry L. Sawyer, CSR #59761, email: ssawyer@sftc.org. (D302)

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Apr-21-2016 Continued To Jan-17-17 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Feb-09-2016 Set For Trial On Aug-29-16 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

OBJECTION HEARING CALENDAR
Off Calendar Feb-24-2016 Off Calendar

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jan-21-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel or Interested Person.

The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT SAN FRANCISCO UNIFIED SCHOOL DISTRICT'S DEMURRER TO PLAINTIFF'S 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT - The demurrer to the disability discrimination cause of action is overruled. The court liberally construes the complaint pursuant to CCP 452. The court can infer that defendant had knowledge of the disability based on plaintiff's allegations about the worker's compensation claim. The complaint states a cause of action and defendant is raising a factual issue. Avila v. Continental Airlines, Inc. (2008) 165 Cal.App.4th 1237, 1248, is distinguishable. That was not a pleading case and it addressed the propriety of granting summary judgment. The demurrer to the retaliation cause of action is sustained without leave to amend as conceded. Order signed.

Judge: Ernest Goldsmith. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976. (302/EG)

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT

No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel or Interested Person.

DEFENDANT SAN FRANCISCO UNIFIED SCHOOL DISTRICT's DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is
continued to January 21, 2016 at 9:30 a.m., in Dept. 302, to give the Plaintiff the opportunity to comply with Local Rule 2.6B. Plaintiff must deliver courtesy copies of the opposition papers to Department 302, with a cover letter stating the new hearing date, no later than January 7, 2016, at 4:00 pm. Any exhibit must be separated by an exhibit tab per CRC 3.1110(f). The court has copies of the moving papers.

Judge: Ernest Goldsmith. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Not reported. (302/EG)
